name: Natural Park of Serra Gelada — Circular from Benidorm
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada — Circular desde Benidorm
region_intro: Hiking around Benidorm offers diverse natural landscapes, combining coastal beauty with mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by impressive cliffs of the Serra Gelada Natural Park, which rise over 300 meters, and the prominent Puig Campana mountain. These features provide a variety of hiking experiences, from coastal paths with sea views to more challenging mountain ascents. The area's geology includes ancient fossil dunes and a rich marine reserve along its protected coastline. Best hiking trails around Benidorm The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of Serra Gelada — Circular from Benidorm, a 12.1 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the coastline and the Serra Gelada cliffs. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Benidorm Island – Tower of Les Caletes loop from Benidorm, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of Benidorm Island and the surrounding bay. Local hikers also love the View of Albir – Governor's Height loop from Camí Coves, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Serra Gelada Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Benidorm is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and protected natural parks like Serra Gelada. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal walks to more difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 6,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Benidorm?
A: Benidorm offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 120 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy coastal walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Benidorm?
A: The trails around Benidorm cater to all abilities. You'll find approximately 68 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes. This variety ensures there's a suitable path whether you're looking for a gentle stroll or a strenuous climb.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Benidorm?
A: Yes, Benidorm has options suitable for families. Many of the easier coastal paths, particularly within the Serra Gelada Natural Park, offer accessible terrain and stunning views without being overly strenuous. For example, the initial section of the Route from La Cruz to Albir Lighthouse is often enjoyed by families.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Benidorm?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5295585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benidorm Island – Tower of Les Caletes loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 12.6 km path offering scenic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14683462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Albir – Governor's Height loop from Camí Coves&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.1 km trail through Serra Gelada Natural Park.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of Benidorm Island?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5295585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benidorm Island – Tower of Les Caletes loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic moderate trail that provides scenic views of Benidorm Island and the surrounding bay. Benidorm Island itself is a protected marine reserve accessible by ferry, offering short trails and birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking near Benidorm?
A: Benidorm's hiking trails offer access to diverse natural attractions. You can explore the impressive cliffs and ancient fossil dunes of the Serra Gelada Natural Park, or hike towards the prominent Puig Campana mountain. For a different experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, located about 15 km away, feature a series of stunning waterfalls and crystal-clear lagoons along the River Algar.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm's mild climate makes it suitable for hiking year-round. However, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months are particularly pleasant, offering comfortable temperatures and less intense sun than the peak summer. This allows for more enjoyable long-distance hikes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes in the Benidorm area?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers routes like the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/497288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the second-highest mountain in the Valencian Community. This can be a demanding 12 km journey with significant elevation gain, rewarding hikers with panoramic views of Benidorm's skyline and the Alicante coastline.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Benidorm?
A: The hiking routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal paths with sea views to challenging mountain trails, and the well-maintained routes within natural parks like Serra Gelada.

Q: Are there any coastal hikes with impressive views?
A: Yes, the Serra Gelada Natural Park is renowned for its coastal paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10329761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint at Pas de la Vella – Serra Gelada Cliffs loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.5 km route that offers extensive views of the coastline and the dramatic Serra Gelada cliffs, which rise over 300 meters high.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Benidorm?
A: Many natural parks and trails in Spain, including some around Benidorm, generally allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary by park or protected area. It's always advisable to check local signage or the park's official website for the most current rules regarding pets before heading out.

Q: Where can I find panoramic viewpoints during a hike?
A: Several trails lead to spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Governor's Height&lt;/a&gt; (Alt del Governador) in Serra Gelada Natural Park, for example, is the highest point at 438 meters and provides panoramic vistas of Benidorm, Altea bays, and the surrounding area. Puig Campana also offers incredible views from its summit.
tips: Panoramic point a few meters from Levante Beach with wonderful views of the city of Benidorm and its impressive beaches with the skyscrapers in the background. A landscape not to be missed!

The Sierra Helada is a Spanish protected natural area located in the province of Alicante, in the Valencian Community. It is part of the network of natural parks of the Valencian Community since July 29, 2005.
The altitude of Alto del Gobernador is about 435 meters. We will reach the top after 2,100 meters of route from the start of the Sierra Helada Natural Park.

The Albir lighthouse is located at the end of the paved road in the Serra Gelada. From here you can see the Bombarda point, the Bombarda tower and the beautiful cliff.

Currently the lighthouse is used as an interpretation center.
The viewpoint has very nice views of the bay and the Sierra de Bernia. You can get to this point very easily by a paved road.
